title: Life expectancy, including UN projections
subtitle: The [period life expectancy](#dod:period-life-expectancy) at birth. This includes the observed life expectancy since 1950, and the medium-variant projections for the future, based on estimates by the UN Population Division.
caption:
Life expectancy, including UN projections

The period life expectancy at birth. This includes the observed life expectancy since 1950, and the medium-variant projections for the future, based on estimates by the UN Population Division.

The chart displays life expectancy trends from 1950 to 2100 for Japan, Sweden, Brazil, India, and the World average. Solid lines represent observed data from 1950 to roughly 2020, while dotted lines indicate projections extending to 2100. In 1950, values range from approximately 41 years for India to 69 years for Sweden. By 2020, Japan and Sweden reach the highest observed levels, clustering between 82 and 84 years. Brazil and the World average sit lower, between 70 and 76 years, while India remains near 70 years. A sharp dip is visible in the India and World series around 2020, dropping roughly 5 to 10 years before recovering. In the projection period, all series trend upward. Japan is projected to exceed 90 years by 2060 and approach 95 years by 2100. Sweden follows a similar trajectory, reaching roughly 90 years by 2100. Brazil and India show steeper growth rates in the future segment, with Brazil rising from roughly 76 to 88 years and India increasing from roughly 70 to 85 years. The World average line ascends from roughly 73 years in 2020 to approximately 82 years by the end of the century. Throughout the entire timeline, the gap between the highest and lowest values narrows, though Japan and Sweden maintain the top positions relative to the other entities shown.
